info.name: Refyne Backend API
info.description: Complete API collection for Refyne Backend with authentication flows and protected routes

request.description: Check if the API is running

item.name: Authentication

request.description: Register a new user account. Password must be at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character.
Request an OTP for passwordless login. Validates credentials first. Note: User must be verified and active.
Login by verifying the OTP. Returns JWT access and refresh tokens.
Refresh the access token using refresh token. Returns new token pair.
Verify user account with verification token (sent via email - not yet implemented)
Request a password reset. In production, a reset link would be sent via email. For development, check server logs or database for the reset token.
Validate a password reset token. Manually set {{reset_token}} variable from database or server logs after requesting password reset.
Reset password using the reset token. After successful reset, use the new password to login.

item.description: Public authentication endpoints - no auth required

item.name: Test Protected Route - Get My Info

request.description: Test endpoint to verify JWT authentication works. Returns authenticated user info.
Logout from current device by blacklisting the current token. Token will be invalid after this.
Logout from all devices. Invalidates current token and marks for multi-device logout.

exec: pm.test("Error message about revoked token", function () {

request.description: This should fail with 401 after logout, proving token blacklist works.

Username must be 3-30 characters

item.name: Phase 5: Audit Logging (DB Verification)

request.description:
Verify audit logs via DB:
SELECT event_type, user_id, ip_address, user_agent, metadata, created_at 
FROM audit_logs 
ORDER BY created_at DESC 
LIMIT 20;

Expected events: user_registered, otp_requested, otp_verified, user_login, user_logout, failed_login_attempt, account_locked

item.description: All auth actions are logged to audit_logs table

Verify 404 errors include request ID

item.name: Phase 7: Token Invalidation

exec: pm.test("Access granted with valid token", function () {

item.name: 7.4 Old Token Should Fail After Password Change

request.description: After password change, old tokens should be invalid due to token_version increment

item.description: Password change invalidates all existing tokens via token_version

item.name: Phase 8: Device Fingerprinting

request.description:
Verify device tracking via DB:
SELECT device_fingerprint, device_name, device_type, browser, os, ip_address, is_suspicious, suspicion_reason, created_at, last_used_at 
FROM device_sessions 
WHERE user_id = '{{user_id}}' 
ORDER BY last_used_at DESC;

Expected: SHA256 fingerprint, parsed device info, suspicious flag

Verify location tracking:
SELECT ip_address, country, city, login_count, first_seen_at, last_seen_at, is_trusted 
FROM login_locations 
WHERE user_id = '{{user_id}}' 
ORDER BY last_seen_at DESC;

Expected: IP tracking, login counts, trusted flag

To test suspicious login:
1. Login from different User-Agent and different IP (use VPN or proxy)
2. Header: X-Forwarded-For: 203.0.113.45
3. Check device_sessions table for is_suspicious = true
4. Check suspicion_reason field

item.description: Device fingerprinting tracks logins and detects suspicious activity

item.name: Phase 9: CORS Protection

request.description: Allowed origin should receive CORS headers
Preflight requests should return CORS headers

item.description: CORS headers protect against unauthorized cross-origin requests

item.name: Phase 10: Database Security

exec: pm.test("Detailed health returns pool stats", function () {

request.description:
Verify connection pool limits:
- Dev: max 10 connections
- Prod: max 20 connections
- Connections are reused and closed properly

Test query timeout (via psql):

-- This should timeout after 30 seconds
SELECT pg_sleep(60);
-- Expected: ERROR: canceling statement due to statement timeout

-- Connection string includes:
-- connect_timeout=10 (connection timeout)
-- statement_timeout=30000 (query timeout in ms)

Monitor connections (via psql):

SELECT 
    count(*) as total_connections,
    count(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'active') as active,
    count(*) FILTER (WHERE state = 'idle') as idle,
    usename
FROM pg_stat_activity 
WHERE datname = 'refyneDB'
GROUP BY usename;

-- Verify connections stay within pool limits

item.description: Database security: restricted user, connection pooling, timeouts
Comprehensive Phase 1.5 Security Testing - All 10 Features
